claim: This Experimental source contract covers the semantic-table tracer, optional controlled or adapter-persisted native column visibility, independently optional bounded native column sizing and semantic detail rows, query-operation layers, and the side-effect-free safe CSV serializer only. It makes no Stable, complete Risk Class 3, interactive-grid, publication, or manual assistive-technology claim.

semantics.nativeBasis: A labelled overflow region contains a native table with a caption, scoped column headers, native buttons for sorting, pagination, and optional detail disclosure, native inputs for filtering, selection, and optional bounded column sizing, and native hidden inputs for optional form serialization.
semantics.rolePolicy: Semantic Table mode remains the default. Optional native column-visibility disclosure and its independently optional persistence adapter, bounded column-sizing ranges, semantic detail rows, filter, pagination, summary, status, retry, selection, and form outputs are absent when their corresponding APIs are disabled; this phase does not expose an ARIA grid role.
semantics.refTarget: The public ref targets the labelled HTMLDivElement region.

keyboard.behavior: Moves through the filter, sortable headers, native selection controls, retry action, and pagination controls in document order.
Activates the focused native sort, retry, pagination, or detail-disclosure button; Space changes the focused native selection control.

Use native radio-group navigation when single-row selection is enabled and adjust a focused enabled native column-sizing range by its declared step. No cell-navigation model is claimed in Semantic Table mode.
Focus remains on an enabled operation control when it survives the refresh and otherwise returns to the labelled grid region.

nonColor: Text, native state, border geometry, aria-sort, selected controls, and explicit status copy accompany every green or violet signal.
responsive: The labelled region remains keyboard-scrollable at narrow widths, controls wrap in logical order, semantic detail rows provide an optional contextual expansion path, coarse-pointer targets reach 44px, and RTL uses logical properties. A priority-column or item-view alternative remains a completion delta.
consumerResponsibilities: Provide stable unique row IDs, localized captions, region labels, operation messages, and task-specific row-selection labels.
In manual mode, apply remote filtering, sorting, and pagination outside the component and return rows and cursor boundaries that match the controlled query.
Keep query adapters deterministic and side-effect safe; transport, routing, persistence, authorization, and retry effects remain consumer-owned.

Supply the exact row model and explicit typed columns for CSV serialization; downloads, storage, network behavior, and any decision to disable default spreadsheet-formula protection remain consumer-owned.

Supply localized column visibility labels where stable IDs are not suitable for user-facing checkbox text; controlled visibility maps must retain at least one known column, and persistence adapters must remain consumer-owned and deterministic.

Declare positive finite min, max, default, and optional step values for every column sizing contract; controlled or default width maps may name only declared sizing columns and must stay within those bounds.
Provide localized detail labels and consumer-owned detail content; controlled or default expanded-row lists may name only unique current source row IDs.
Preserve native table structure and retest long content, 320px reflow, 400% zoom, RTL, touch, forced colors, reduced motion, and supported assistive technology.
Do not claim Stable or complete Data Grid conformance until the recorded completion delta and Risk Class 3 manual matrix are closed.
